q: Thermoreceptors in the skin detect a drop in ambient temperature. Which structure integrates this information and coordinates the response?
options: The cerebellum, which adjusts muscle tone to generate heat
The hypothalamus, which acts as the body's thermostat and triggers corrective responses
The adrenal medulla, which releases cortisol to raise the set point
The pancreas, which releases glucagon to increase metabolic heat
explanation: The hypothalamus receives input from peripheral and central thermoreceptors and coordinates responses (shivering, vasoconstriction, etc.) to maintain core temperature.
